claims.evidence_text: Among the F 3 wheat populations, the WUE gy ranged from 0.05 g mm −1 (LM47 X LM70) to 0.21 g mm −1 (BW141 X LM71) under DS conditions
claims.claim_summary: Grain-yield water productivity (WUEgy) of F3 wheat populations ranged 0.05-0.21 g/mm under drought-stressed conditions

claims.evidence_text: the WUE gy was lower under drought-stressed (0.11 g mm −1 ) than non-stressed conditions (0.13 g mm −1 )
claims.claim_summary: Mean grain-yield water productivity of wheat was 0.11 g/mm under drought stress vs 0.13 g/mm non-stressed

claims.evidence_text: the earliest maturing genotypes under drought stress included LM26 X BW140 and LM48 X BW141 and reached DTM at 95.00 and 95.25 days... under non-stressed, the genotypes LM47 X LM70 and BW152 X LM70 reached DTM at 94.00 and 95.67 days
claims.claim_summary: Earliest-maturing wheat genotypes reached 50% maturity at about 94-95.7 days after sowing

claims.evidence_text: The overall mean for plant height (PH) was higher under drought stress (81.09 cm) than non-stressed (80.43 cm).
claims.claim_summary: Mean wheat plant height was ~81 cm (80.4-81.1 cm across water regimes)

claims.evidence_text: The R:S varied from 0.11 (LM26) to 0.29 (BW152 X LM47) under a drought-stressed environment, whilst under non-stressed conditions, it ranged between 0.09 (LM75 X LM48) to 0.29 (LM47)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat root-to-shoot ratio ranged 0.09-0.29 across genotypes and water regimes (mean ~0.18-0.19)

claims.evidence_text: the mean HI under drought-stressed conditions was 18.32%, which was lower than the HI under non-stressed conditions, which was 20.92%.
claims.claim_summary: Mean wheat harvest index was 18.3% under drought stress and 20.9% non-stressed

claims.evidence_text: Basal fertilizer was applied at 120, 30, and 30 kg ha −1 of nitrogen, phosphorous, and potassium, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Basal nitrogen applied to wheat at 120 kg/ha

claims.evidence_text: Basal fertilizer was applied at 120, 30, and 30 kg ha −1 of nitrogen, phosphorous, and potassium, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Basal phosphorus applied to wheat at 30 kg/ha

claims.evidence_text: Basal fertilizer was applied at 120, 30, and 30 kg ha −1 of nitrogen, phosphorous, and potassium, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Basal potassium applied to wheat at 30 kg/ha

claims.evidence_text: The plot size was a 1 m-long plot with an inter-row spacing of 30 cm and intra-row spacing of 20 cm.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat inter-row spacing of 30 cm (intra-row 20 cm) used in the trial

claims.evidence_text: Limited and variable rainfall conditions during flowering and grain filling stages remain the leading cause of poor yields and quality... water stress was imposed... by withholding irrigation to 35% field capacity from 50% heading to physiological maturity.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat flowering and grain-filling stages (heading to physiological maturity) are the drought-sensitive period for yield and quality

claims.evidence_text: Drought stress significantly reduce grain yield production of wheat genotypes... The mean grain yield value of the F 3 families was higher by 29.42% and WUE by 25.00% than the parental lines under DS conditions.
claims.claim_summary: Drought stress significantly reduces wheat grain yield; drought-tolerant water-use-efficient families recommended for water-limited agro-ecologies
